title: MIDI Piano Motion Tools Pro
description:
🎹 MIDI Piano Motion Tools Pro

Version: 2.0 | Blender 4.2+ | Category: Animation

🧠 What It Does

MIDI Piano Motion Tools Pro turns your MIDI files into fully-animated 3D piano performances inside Blender. It supports velocity-sensitive key movements, dynamic lighting, pedal and hammer animations, and even physics-based falling ball effects. Whether you're creating content for YouTube, teaching piano online, or crafting musical visualizations, this add-on handles the complexity—so you can focus on creativity.

PRO MAX Version:https://superhivemarket.com/products/midi-piano-motion-tools-pro-max---transform-music-into-visual-magic




🔧 Main Features

✅ Automatic MIDI Animation System

  • Frame-accurate key animation driven by MIDI velocity

  • Realistic hammer and pedal movement (damper, sostenuto, una corda)

  • Configurable animation timing, velocity sensitivity, and frame offsets

  • Fully supports pitch bend, modulation wheel, transposition, and channel filtering

💡 Dynamic Visual Effects

  • Responsive key emission lighting

  • Per-hand color control and customizable emission strength

  • Custom key color mapping for each MIDI note

  • Gradient-based color system with direction and interpolation control


🔴 Physics-Based Falling Balls

  • Simulated balls fall onto keys with accurate gravity and bounce

  • Emission animation on impact, fade-out, and customizable trajectory

  • Fully adjustable: size, bounce height, timing offsets, and glow




🎼 Advanced MIDI Controls

  • Select specific tracks and channels

  • Import only selected time ranges from a MIDI file

  • Realtime MIDI info panel shows BPM, note count, duration, and per-channel stats

🛠️ Real-Time Editing Tools

  • Panel in 3D View Sidebar (View3D > Sidebar > Piano)

  • Live testing for object mappings (keys, hammers, pedals)

  • Clear/reset all animations and materials with one click

  • UI lists and eyedropper tools for managing custom key colors

📦 Installation

  1. Download the ZIP file

  2. Install via Blender Preferences > Add-ons > Install

  3. Enable the add-on

  4. Open the Piano tab in the 3D View sidebar

🖥️ Requirements

  • Blender 3.6 or later

  • A compatible 3D piano model (not included)

  • Standard MIDI file (.mid)

  • Mido library (already bundled inside libs folder)

🌈 Ideal For

  • YouTube music animations

  • Online piano educators

  • Artistic visualizers

  • Performance visuals

  • Piano model demo reels

⭐ Reviews

"This is hands-down the most complete MIDI animation tool for Blender."
Dennis Denny — Animation Director, ★★★★★

"Used it to generate stunning visuals for my live piano sessions—game changer!"
Michael — Blender Musician, ★★★★★

💰 Pricing

Single User License – $30.00

  • Lifetime updates

  • Commercial use included

  • Priority support

🚀 Why Choose MIDI Piano Motion Tools Pro?

  • Full-featured yet intuitive

  • Customizable to your unique style

  • Professional-grade results with zero manual keyframing

  • Active development and support from a real Blender user

📞 Need Help?

Support is just a click away—contact the creator directly through Blender Market or email: qq475519905@gmail.com


Support Blender Foundation

We believe in giving back to the community that makes our work possible. Every month, we dedicate a portion of our plugin's revenue to support the Blender Foundation. Thank you for helping us contribute to the future of open-source creativity.
documentation:
PianoMotion Pro

User Manual

1. Introduction

PianoMotion Pro is an animation assistant add-on for Blender that enables the import of standard MIDI files into Blender, automatically generating realistic animations for piano key presses, pedal movements, hammer actions, and optional effects like falling balls and key illumination. It's perfect for:

  • Music Animation Creators: Quickly produce synchronized piano performance visualizations.

  • Education/Demonstrations: Highlight rhythm and note positions with glowing keys or falling balls.

  • 3D Artists: Eliminate the tedious process of manually animating each keyframe, allowing more focus on design and camera work.

This manual will guide you through installation, UI functionality, and a complete usage workflow, helping you create perfectly synchronized music animations with ease.

2. Installation

2.1 Add-on Installation

Install the .zip via Blender Preferences:

  • Obtain the .zip file, e.g., MIDI_PianoMotion_Tools.zip, from the website or the developer.

  • In Blender, go to Edit > Preferences > Add-ons.

  • Click Install, select the .zip file, and click “Install Add-on”.

  • In the add-on list, find PianoMotion Pro and enable it by ticking the checkbox.

2.2 mido Library Dependency

mido is a Python library for handling MIDI files, required by PianoMotion Pro.

If you see an error like "No module named 'mido'", it means the library is missing.

Solutions:

  • In Blender's Python Console, run:

import pip
pip.main(['install', 'mido'])

Or:

  • Install mido using your system Python, then copy the module into Blender’s scripts/modules folder.

Restart Blender. If successful, MIDI import will work correctly.

After installation, press N in the 3D Viewport and go to the Piano tab to access the plugin panel.

3. User Interface Overview

When enabled, the add-on panel appears in the 3D Viewport under the Piano tab (N key). The interface is organized into multiple sections:

  • MIDI File Info (shown after successful import)

  • Animation Components

  • Animation Timing

  • MIDI Controller Settings

  • Key Settings

  • Key Colors & Custom Key Colors

  • Hammer Settings

  • Pedal Settings

  • Emission Settings

  • Ball Settings

  • Test Components & Clear Animations

  • Create Animation

4. Feature Overview

4.1 MIDI File Info

  • BPM: Beats per minute (tempo).

  • Note Count: Total number of notes in the MIDI file.

  • Duration: Playback length in seconds.

  • Channel Info: Lists detected channels with note count, instruments, and velocity ranges.

Useful for identifying correct tracks in multi-channel MIDI files.

4.2 Animation Components

  • Enable Key Animation: Creates press/release animations for piano keys.

  • Enable Hammer Animation: Animates internal hammers to simulate real mechanical movement (requires correct naming).

  • Enable Pedal Animation: Auto-generates keyframes for pedals based on MIDI messages (64, 66, 67).

  • Enable Falling Balls: Adds Synthesia-style falling balls for notes.

  • Enable Key Emission: Makes keys glow when pressed and fade when released.

4.3 Animation Timing

  • Start Frame: Frame where animation begins on the timeline.

  • Key Press Duration: Number of frames a key stays pressed visually (independent from actual note duration).

  • Velocity Influence: Weight (0–1) of note velocity affecting animation length and speed.

4.4 MIDI Controller Settings

  • Use Pitch Bend: Enables MIDI pitch wheel effects (e.g., glide).

  • Pitch Bend Range: Max semitone range for bend (e.g., 2–4 recommended).

  • Use Modulation: Enables modulation wheel effects.

  • Modulation Effect:

    • VIBRATO: Slight vibration of keys

    • EMISSION: Emission brightness control

    • BOTH: Both vibrato and brightness change

Note: Requires actual modulation data in the MIDI file.

4.5 Key Settings

  • White Key Prefix / Black Key Prefix: Used to identify white and black key object names.

  • Key Separator: Character between prefix and number (e.g., ., _).

Example: WhiteKey.001 → Prefix = WhiteKey, Separator = .

4.6 Key Colors & Custom Key Colors

(1) Key Colors

  • White/Black Key Color: Sets base color of keys.

  • Roughness: Material roughness (0 = smooth, 1 = rough).

(2) Custom Key Colors

  • Use Custom Key Colors: Enables per-note glow colors (for notes 21–108).

  • Assign specific colors using the mapping or eyedropper tool.

4.7 Hammer Settings

  • Hammer Prefix: Naming prefix for hammer objects (e.g., pCube).

  • Hammer Movement: Distance the hammer moves when triggered.

4.8 Pedal Settings

  • Damper / Sostenuto / Una Corda Pedal: Object names for pedal animation.

  • Pedal Rotation: Rotation angle around the X-axis when pressed.

4.9 Emission Settings

  • Emission Strength: Brightness when a key is pressed.

  • Fade Frames: Number of frames until glow fades out.

  • Use Gradient Colors: Enables color gradients based on direction.

  • Gradient Direction: Horizontal / Vertical / Single key.

  • Gradient Stops: Define color stops for the gradient.

If not using gradients, glow color is assigned by hand (left/right) using split_note + emission_color.

4.10 Ball Settings

(1) Main Parameters

  • Ball Size: Radius of each ball.

  • Start Height: Initial Y-position above keys.

  • Bounce Height: Rebound height after impact.

(2) Physics

  • Bounce Force: Multiplier for ball rebound.

  • Backward Force: Horizontal movement.

  • Vertical Factor: Larger value = more vertical bounce.

(3) Animation

  • Bounce Duration / Time: Timing for the bounce animation.

  • Fade Time: Frames until ball shrinks and disappears.

(4) Emission

  • Emission Offset: Glow effect timing shift.

  • Timing Offset: Fall time offset from note press.

  • Collision Brightness / Falling Brightness: Glow intensity on impact and during fall.

4.11 Test Components & Clear Animations

Test Components:
Checks if objects (keys, pedals, hammers) are properly named and detected.
Warnings will indicate missing parts, allowing you to correct them beforehand.

Clear Animations:
Deletes all animations, objects, and materials generated by the plugin.
Useful when importing a new MIDI or changing settings.

4.12 Create Animation

Main button to execute the process:

  • Opens file browser to select .mid file.

  • Parses MIDI and generates keyframes according to panel settings.

  • Timeline and Action Editor will display the new animations.

  • A message will confirm success, e.g., “Animation created! Duration: 5:12”.

5. Workflow

1. Preparation

  • Ensure the scene contains a piano model named correctly (keys, hammers, pedals).

  • Apply transforms (Ctrl + A) to normalize axes and origins.

  • Save a backup .blend before testing.

2. Test Components

  • Use the “Test Components” button to detect missing or misnamed parts.

3. Import MIDI

  • Click “Create Animation” and select a .mid file.

  • Choose specific tracks/channels or transpose if needed.

4. Review & Adjust

  • Play the animation and inspect keys, pedals, balls, and emission.

  • If changes are needed (e.g., duration, colors), Clear Animation and Create Animation again.

5. Finalize

  • Repeat as needed until satisfied.

  • Add camera, lighting, and export the final animation.

6. FAQ

6.1 No animation?

  • Check if mido is installed.

  • Use “Test Components” to find misnamed objects.

  • Try converting problematic MIDI to standard format using external software.

6.2 Keys not glowing?

  • Ensure “Enable Key Emission” is checked.

  • Increase emission strength or enable Bloom in Eevee.

  • Confirm correct material connections.

6.3 Partial animation only?

  • Use "Time Range" in the import dialog to set duration.

  • Delete excess keyframes manually in the Action Editor.

  • Limit to specific tracks/channels during import.

6.4 Balls not showing?

  • Confirm “Enable Falling Balls” is enabled.

  • Adjust ball size and ball height if invisible.

  • Check if selected tracks contain note data.

6.5 Adjusting start frame or speed?

  • Change Start Frame or Tempo Multiplier in the import dialog.

  • Minor tweaks can be made by moving keyframes, but major changes should use the panel options.

7. Requirements

Piano Model Specs

  • 52 White Keys

  • 36 Black Keys

  • 88 Hammers

  • 3 Pedals

Naming Rules

  • White Keys: meshId57_name.1 to .52

  • Black Keys: meshId56_name.1 to .36

  • Hammers: pCube1 to pCube88

  • Pedals: piano_pedal_metal01, metal02, metal03

Performance Tips

  • Larger MIDI files take longer to process.

  • Test with short MIDI files for efficiency.

8. Conclusion

With this manual, you now have the tools to turn MIDI files into stunning piano animations using PianoMotion Pro. Whether for teaching, visualization, or artistic projects, this plugin simplifies a complex task into a creative workflow.

For advanced features or further customization, refer to other documentation or join discussions in the community.

Happy animating, and may your creations shine!

Tip: Add UI screenshots or rendered images throughout this manual to enhance clarity and understanding.

9. Technical Support
